content.body: Metals are extracted from their ores using different methods depending on their position in the reactivity series.

REACTIVITY SERIES (high→low): K, Na, Ca, Mg, Al, Zn, Fe, Pb, Cu, Ag, Au
Mnemonic: 'King Solomon Can Make A Zip Fastener, Like Copper Silver And Gold'

EXTRACTION METHODS:
• Very reactive metals (K, Na, Ca, Mg, Al): Electrolysis
• Moderately reactive (Zn, Fe, Pb): Reduction with carbon/coke
• Unreactive metals (Cu, Ag, Au): Found native or simple heating

Reduction with carbon: Metal oxide + Carbon → Metal + CO₂
Example: ZnO + C → Zn + CO (at high temperature)

content.title: Iron from Blast Furnace
content.body:
Raw materials: iron ore (haematite Fe₂O₃), coke (C), limestone (CaCO₃), hot air
Key reactions:
1. C + O₂ → CO₂ (coke burns)
2. CO₂ + C → 2CO (carbon monoxide formed)
3. Fe₂O₃ + 3CO → 2Fe + 3CO₂ (iron reduced)
4. CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂ (limestone decomposes)
5. CaO + SiO₂ → CaSiO₃ (slag removes impurities)

questions.question: Aluminium is extracted by electrolysis rather than carbon reduction because:
options: Aluminium is more reactive than carbon
questions.answer: Aluminium is more reactive than carbon
questions.explanation: Carbon can only reduce metal oxides of metals LESS reactive than carbon. Al is more reactive than C.

questions.question: Iron is extracted from its ore in a:
questions.explanation: The blast furnace uses coke (carbon) to reduce iron oxide (Fe₂O₃) to iron at very high temperatures.

questions.question: The reducing agent in the blast furnace is:
questions.explanation: CO is the main reducing agent: Fe₂O₃ + 3CO → 2Fe + 3CO₂

questions.question: The purpose of limestone in the blast furnace is:
options: To remove acidic silica impurities as slag
questions.answer: To remove acidic silica impurities as slag
questions.explanation: CaCO₃ → CaO which reacts with SiO₂ → CaSiO₃ (slag). Slag floats on iron and is removed.

questions.question: Which metal can be found in pure (native) form in the Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Gold is unreactive — it doesn't combine with other elements, so it exists as pure gold in nature.

questions.question: Electrolysis is used to extract:
questions.explanation: Very reactive metals (above carbon in reactivity series) require electrolysis: Na, K, Ca, Mg, Al.

questions.question: In the reactivity series, which metal is MOST reactive?
questions.explanation: Potassium (K) is at the top of the reactivity series — most reactive common metal.

questions.question: Haematite is an ore of:
questions.explanation: Haematite = Fe₂O₃ (iron(III) oxide) — the main ore used in iron extraction.

questions.question: Reduction of metal oxides with carbon works when:
options: Metal is more reactive than carbon
Metal is less reactive than carbon
questions.answer: Metal is less reactive than carbon
questions.explanation: Carbon reduces metals below it in reactivity. Carbon cannot reduce oxides of K, Na, Ca, Mg, Al.

questions.question: The ore of aluminium is:
questions.explanation: Bauxite (Al₂O₃·2H₂O) is the main ore from which aluminium is extracted by electrolysis.

questions.question: Slag formed in blast furnace is:
questions.explanation: CaO + SiO₂ → CaSiO₃ (slag). Slag is less dense than iron, floats on top, and is tapped off separately.

questions.question: Which is NOT a raw material for the blast furnace?
questions.explanation: The three raw materials are iron ore, coke (carbon), and limestone. NaCl has no role.

questions.question: Iron produced from a blast furnace is called pig iron. It contains:
questions.explanation: Blast furnace iron (pig iron/cast iron) is hard but brittle, containing about 4% carbon.

questions.question: To make steel from pig iron, you:
questions.explanation: Steel has 0.1–1.5% carbon. Excess carbon is removed by blowing oxygen through molten pig iron.

questions.question: Malachite (Cu₂(OH)₂CO₃) is an ore of:
questions.explanation: Malachite is a green copper-containing mineral — an ore of copper.

questions.question: During electrolysis of aluminium oxide (Al₂O₃), aluminium forms at the:
questions.explanation: Al³⁺ ions are attracted to cathode (negative electrode) and are reduced: Al³⁺ + 3e⁻ → Al

questions.question: Which gas is produced at the anode during electrolysis of Al₂O₃?
questions.explanation: O²⁻ ions are oxidised at anode: 2O²⁻ → O₂ + 4e⁻

questions.question: Zinc oxide + carbon → zinc + carbon dioxide. This is:
questions.explanation: ZnO is reduced (loses O) by carbon. This is a redox reaction where carbon is the reducing agent.

questions.question: Why is iron more commonly used than aluminium despite aluminium being stronger?
options: Iron is cheaper to extract (blast furnace vs electrolysis)
questions.answer: Iron is cheaper to extract (blast furnace vs electrolysis)
questions.explanation: Electrolysis (for Al) uses huge amounts of electricity, making aluminium more expensive to produce than iron.

questions.question: The reactivity series helps predict:
options: Which metals can be extracted by carbon and which need electrolysis
questions.answer: Which metals can be extracted by carbon and which need electrolysis
questions.explanation: Position in reactivity series directly determines the extraction method required.
